_comment: Edit this file directly or run scripts/generate_archetypes.py. See docs/EDITING_DATA.md

commander.name: Commander
commander.tagline: Driven to achieve goals through determination and high standards
commander.core_wound: Confuses control with care; equates softness with weakness
commander.description: Commanders are driven to achieve goals through determination and holding themselves and others to high standards of performance.

questions.identity: What does being a Commander feel like from the inside — what quality in yourself are you most proud of that others rarely see?
questions.memory: Describe a moment when your determination either saved something important — or cost you something you didn't expect to lose.
questions.unsaid: What do you know about yourself as a leader that you have never admitted out loud, even to yourself?
questions.fear: When was the last time you let someone see you lose — and what did it cost you to hide it?

commander.image_direction: A lone general standing in an empty war room, maps on the table, no one left to command, single light source
commander.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

shaper.name: Shaper
shaper.tagline: Visualizes ambitious goals and pushes through relentlessly
shaper.core_wound: Self-worth is entirely fused with the magnitude of their output
shaper.description: Shapers visualize ambitious goals and push through obstacles with relentless drive, measuring themselves by the scale of what they build.

questions.identity: When you picture your best self, what are you holding in your hands — and who is watching?
questions.memory: Describe a project you pushed through that proved something to you — and whether it was worth what it took.
questions.unsaid: What would you have to admit if your current ambition failed tomorrow?
questions.fear: What are you building right now that you secretly suspect you're doing to prove something — not because you actually want it?

shaper.image_direction: A sculptor chiseling away at a statue that keeps growing larger than the room
shaper.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

quietleader.name: Quiet Leader
quietleader.core_wound: Carries enormous internal clarity that almost never gets spoken aloud
quietleader.description: Quiet Leaders lead through open-mindedness and equanimity, guiding others with calm presence rather than forceful direction.

questions.identity: What quality of stillness in yourself do you trust most when everything around you is loud?
questions.memory: Describe a time your quiet leadership changed an outcome — and whether anyone knew it was you.
questions.unsaid: What would change in your life if you spoke with half the clarity you think with?
questions.fear: What is something true about your situation that you have fully understood but never said out loud to anyone — and why haven't you said it?

quietleader.image_direction: A lighthouse, fully lit, surrounded by fog — ships guided but never seeing the source
quietleader.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

inspirer.name: Inspirer
inspirer.tagline: Motivates people to get behind challenging ideas
inspirer.core_wound: Believes in the vision so hard they forget to check if others actually followed
inspirer.description: Inspirers motivate people to get behind challenging ideas, carrying conviction that can outpace the room's actual commitment.

questions.identity: What part of you lights up when someone finally grasps the vision you've been carrying?
questions.memory: Describe a moment you rallied people — and the first sign you weren't sure they were with you.
questions.unsaid: What would you stop doing if you couldn't inspire anyone this week?
questions.fear: Who are you inspiring right now who you secretly fear isn't as committed as you need them to be?

inspirer.image_direction: A conductor mid-symphony, turning to find half the orchestra has left their chairs
inspirer.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

campaigner.name: Campaigner
campaigner.tagline: Rallies others around ideas and achieves practical results
campaigner.core_wound: Momentum becomes identity; stopping feels like dying
campaigner.description: Campaigners rally others around ideas and drive toward practical results, often finding that motion itself becomes who they are.

questions.identity: What does momentum feel like in your body when a cause is actually working?
questions.memory: Describe a campaign that succeeded — and whether you felt emptied or alive afterward.
questions.unsaid: Who would you be if you stopped campaigning for six months?
questions.fear: What cause or mission are you still fighting for that you privately wonder if you even believe in anymore?

campaigner.image_direction: A lone figure still holding a banner in an empty field after the crowd has gone
campaigner.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

coach.name: Coach
coach.tagline: Focused on self-growth, development and teaching others
coach.core_wound: Identity built entirely around being needed; forgets their own needs exist
coach.description: Coaches are focused on growth, development, and teaching others — often building identity around being the one who holds things together.

questions.identity: What do you love most about watching someone else grow — and when did you last feel that for yourself?
questions.memory: Describe a time you coached someone brilliantly and neglected yourself in the same week.
questions.unsaid: What need of yours have you reframed as 'just helping others'?
questions.fear: Think of the person you're currently carrying the most weight for. What part of yourself have you quietly set aside to make room for them?

coach.image_direction: A pair of hands holding up a growing tree, roots slowly wrapping around the wrists
coach.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

promoter.name: Promoter
promoter.core_wound: The performance of enthusiasm can become a prison — exhausting to maintain, terrifying to drop
promoter.description: Promoters are outgoing and charismatic, skilled at energizing rooms — but the performance of enthusiasm can become exhausting to maintain.

questions.identity: What version of you shows up when you don't have to win anyone over?
questions.memory: Describe a time the room went quiet and you weren't sure who you were without the applause.
questions.unsaid: What would people think if they saw you on a day you couldn't perform?
questions.fear: When you are completely alone and the performance is off — what does the silence feel like?

promoter.image_direction: A stage with one spotlight still lit, audience gone, performer sitting at the edge
promoter.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

impresario.name: Impresario
impresario.tagline: Entertains, engages socially, facilitates great experiences
impresario.core_wound: Confuses the quality of the experience they create with the quality of their own worth
impresario.description: Impresarios entertain, engage socially, and facilitate memorable experiences — sometimes mistaking the quality of what they create for their own worth.

questions.identity: When you create a perfect evening for others, what are you hoping they'll feel about you?
questions.memory: Describe an event you orchestrated that went beautifully — and how you felt when everyone left.
questions.unsaid: Who would still want you around if you offered nothing but your presence?
questions.fear: What would happen to your relationships if you stopped making things memorable for people?

impresario.image_direction: An empty banquet hall, perfectly set, waiting for guests who may not come
impresario.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

entertainer.name: Entertainer
entertainer.tagline: Creates experiences and engages with the world
entertainer.core_wound: Humor and performance as armor — the joke is always ready before the vulnerability can surface
entertainer.description: Entertainers create experiences and engage with the world through performance — often with humor ready before vulnerability can surface.

questions.identity: What do you feel when the room is laughing — and what do you feel when the room goes quiet?
questions.memory: Describe a moment you made everyone laugh and went home feeling hollow.
questions.unsaid: What truth have you never told because you always had a punchline ready?
questions.fear: What's the thing you deflect with a laugh that, if you sat with it quietly, would actually break you open?

entertainer.image_direction: A jester mask hanging on a nail, the face behind it blurred and undefined
entertainer.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

peacekeeper.name: Peacekeeper
peacekeeper.tagline: Develops positive relationships, seeks harmony and cooperation
peacekeeper.core_wound: Conflict-avoidance masquerading as kindness; they disappear themselves to keep the room comfortable
peacekeeper.description: Peacekeepers develop positive relationships and seek harmony, sometimes disappearing themselves to keep rooms comfortable.

questions.identity: When harmony is restored, what part of you feels most relieved — and what part feels most erased?
questions.memory: Describe a time you kept the peace and lost something true about yourself in the process.
questions.unsaid: What disagreement are you avoiding that would actually set you free?
questions.fear: What do you actually think — about a situation you've been nodding through — that you have never let yourself say?

peacekeeper.image_direction: A bridge over turbulent water, perfectly maintained, slightly cracked at the center
peacekeeper.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

problemsolver.name: Problem Solver
problemsolver.tagline: Supports and helps others in an industrious, professional way
problemsolver.core_wound: Helping is safe; being helped is terrifying — receiving care feels like losing ground
problemsolver.description: Problem Solvers support others industriously and professionally — finding that helping is safe while being helped feels like losing ground.

questions.identity: What problem do you solve for others that you wish someone would solve for you?
questions.memory: Describe a time you fixed everything for everyone and collapsed when no one noticed.
questions.unsaid: What would it cost your identity to let someone take care of you?
questions.fear: Who in your life keeps offering you something — support, rest, help — that you keep refusing? What are you protecting by refusing it?

problemsolver.image_direction: A toolbox overflowing, the hands holding it bleeding slightly at the palms
problemsolver.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

helper.name: Helper
helper.tagline: Compassion, care, support of emotional needs
helper.core_wound: Love expressed through service rather than presence — giving so no one looks too closely at what they need
helper.description: Helpers express love through service and emotional care — giving so generously that no one looks too closely at what they need.

questions.identity: When you help someone, what do you hope they'll never have to see about you?
questions.memory: Describe a time you gave everything you had and still felt unseen.
questions.unsaid: What do you need that you've disguised as generosity?
questions.fear: If the person you love most could only receive one true thing from you — not help, not effort, just you — what would that be, and why is it so hard to give?

helper.image_direction: An open door with warm light spilling out, the person standing just outside it, never stepping in
helper.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

strategist.name: Strategist
strategist.tagline: Generates and translates concepts into effective strategies
strategist.core_wound: Intelligence used as distance — thinking about the problem instead of feeling it
strategist.description: Strategists generate concepts and translate them into effective strategies — sometimes using intelligence as distance from feeling.

questions.identity: When you see the whole board clearly, what feeling do you push aside to keep thinking?
questions.memory: Describe a strategy you mapped brilliantly that you never had the courage to execute.
questions.unsaid: What are you afraid would happen if you stopped analyzing and simply moved?
questions.fear: What is the thing in your life you have analyzed perfectly and acted on least — and what does that gap tell you?

strategist.image_direction: An intricate chessboard fully solved, the player staring at it, unable to make the first move
strategist.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

planner.name: Planner
planner.tagline: Structure, systems, translating ideas into plans
planner.core_wound: Certainty addiction — the plan is a defense against the terror of irreversible action
planner.description: Planners create structure and systems, translating ideas into plans — sometimes using certainty as defense against irreversible action.

questions.identity: What does a perfect plan give you that acting never could?
questions.memory: Describe a plan you refined for months that became a reason never to begin.
questions.unsaid: What decision are you postponing by calling it 'not ready yet'?
questions.fear: What are you still planning that would feel unsafe even if everything went exactly right?

planner.image_direction: An architect's blueprints spread across a table, the foundation already crumbling beneath it
planner.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

orchestrator.name: Orchestrator
orchestrator.tagline: Brings people together and mobilizes resources
orchestrator.core_wound: Invisible to themselves in their own system — so focused on moving pieces they forget they're a piece too
orchestrator.description: Orchestrators bring people together and mobilize resources — so focused on moving pieces they forget they're a piece too.

questions.identity: When everything is coordinated, where do you stand in the picture you've built?
questions.memory: Describe a time you held everything together and realized no one was holding you.
questions.unsaid: What would fall apart if you stopped orchestrating for one week?
questions.fear: In all the systems and people you're organizing right now — where do you actually fit? Not as a role. As a person.

orchestrator.image_direction: The center of a clock mechanism, every gear turning, the center axle standing perfectly still and alone
orchestrator.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

implementer.name: Implementer
implementer.tagline: Organizes and structures people and processes to execute
implementer.core_wound: Execution as escape — staying busy means never having to ask if this is what they actually want
implementer.description: Implementers organize people and processes to execute — staying busy can mean never asking if this is what they actually want.

questions.identity: What does finishing something feel like — relief, proof, or escape?
questions.memory: Describe a project you executed flawlessly that left you wondering why you did it.
questions.unsaid: What desire have you buried under the next task list?
questions.fear: If you stopped executing for 30 days and could only think — what question would surface that you've been too busy to ask yourself?

implementer.image_direction: A long corridor of open doors, each completed and closed, one door at the end slightly ajar
implementer.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

investigator.name: Investigator
investigator.tagline: Researches and analyzes to build knowledge
investigator.core_wound: The need to fully understand before acting — knowledge as permission they never quite grant themselves
investigator.description: Investigators research and analyze to build knowledge — needing to fully understand before acting, as if knowledge were permission they never grant.

questions.identity: When you learn something new, what old question does it quiet — and for how long?
questions.memory: Describe the moment you knew enough to act and chose to read one more article instead.
questions.unsaid: What permission are you waiting for that only you can give?
questions.fear: What have you researched deeply and known clearly for a long time — and still not acted on? What are you still looking for?

investigator.image_direction: A researcher at a desk surrounded by answered questions, one small blank piece of paper in the center
investigator.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

technician.name: Technician
technician.tagline: Breaks down, analyzes and solves problems technically
technician.core_wound: Precision as protection — if the mechanism is perfect, the human messiness around it can be ignored
technician.description: Technicians break down and solve problems technically — using precision as protection from human messiness.

questions.identity: When the system works perfectly, what human variable still unsettles you?
questions.memory: Describe a time you fixed the mechanism and the relationship still broke.
questions.unsaid: What mess in your life are you treating as a technical problem?
questions.fear: What problem in your life cannot be solved technically — and how long have you been pretending it can?

technician.image_direction: A perfectly calibrated machine with one organic element — a leaf, a hand — that keeps disrupting it
technician.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

adventurer.name: Adventurer
adventurer.core_wound: Motion as avoidance — as long as you're moving, you never have to arrive anywhere that requires you to stay
adventurer.description: Adventurers seek excitement and new experiences — motion as avoidance of arriving somewhere that requires them to stay.

questions.identity: What does the next adventure promise you that the present cannot?
questions.memory: Describe a trip or thrill that was supposed to change you — and what stayed the same when you returned.
questions.unsaid: What are you running toward that is actually something you're running from?
questions.fear: What would you have to confront about yourself if you stopped moving and stayed in one place for a year?

adventurer.image_direction: A map covered in routes and pins, one location circled and never visited
adventurer.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

artisan.name: Artisan
artisan.tagline: Uses creativity to bring beautiful, well-crafted ideas to life
artisan.core_wound: The work is never quite finished — because finished means judged, and judged means exposed
artisan.description: Artisans use creativity to craft beautiful work — often keeping pieces unfinished because finished means judged, and judged means exposed.

questions.identity: When you refine one more detail, what judgment are you postponing?
questions.memory: Describe something you nearly released and pulled back at the last moment.
questions.unsaid: What would it mean about you if the work were seen and found ordinary?
questions.fear: What is something you've made — or almost made — that you've kept from the world? What are you protecting it from?

artisan.image_direction: A painting covered by a cloth in a lit gallery, every other piece on the walls
artisan.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

inventor.name: Inventor
inventor.tagline: Generates new and innovative ideas, products and solutions
inventor.core_wound: The next idea is always more exciting than the current one — completion is a kind of grief
inventor.description: Inventors generate innovative ideas and solutions — often finding the next idea more exciting than completing the current one.

questions.identity: When a new idea arrives, what does it save you from feeling about the old one?
questions.memory: Describe an invention or project you abandoned the moment something shinier appeared.
questions.unsaid: What would you lose if you finished one thing completely?
questions.fear: What idea have you had — repeatedly, for years — that you have never committed to? What keeps making it feel 'not quite right yet'?

inventor.image_direction: A workbench covered in brilliant half-finished inventions, one window looking out on the world
inventor.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

explorer.name: Explorer
explorer.tagline: Seeks new knowledge and experiences with intrinsic motivation
explorer.core_wound: Possibility addiction — commitment feels like the death of the self that might have been something else
explorer.description: Explorers seek new knowledge and experiences — sometimes treating commitment as the death of selves they might have become.

questions.identity: What does an open door represent to you — freedom, or a way to never be found?
questions.memory: Describe a path you walked far enough to know it was right — and still walked away.
questions.unsaid: Which version of yourself are you protecting by never fully choosing?
questions.fear: What would you lose — about who you think you are — if you finally committed to one path and stopped leaving exits open?

explorer.image_direction: A crossroads with ten paths visible, footprints going a few steps down each one, returning to center
explorer.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

thinker.name: Thinker
thinker.tagline: Takes an abstract, philosophical approach to problems
thinker.core_wound: Living in the mind as a way of avoiding the body and its inconvenient demands
thinker.description: Thinkers take abstract, philosophical approaches — living in the mind as a way of avoiding the body's inconvenient demands.

questions.identity: When you understand something completely in theory, what part of living it frightens you?
questions.memory: Describe an insight that changed how you think but not how you act.
questions.unsaid: What would your body say if your mind stopped speaking for it?
questions.fear: What truth have you arrived at philosophically that your actual daily life completely contradicts?

thinker.image_direction: A figure standing at the edge of a cliff, perfectly calm, a storm raging behind them they haven't noticed
thinker.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

growthseeker.name: Growth Seeker
growthseeker.tagline: Deep passion for learning and personal development
growthseeker.core_wound: Growth as identity — if you stop improving, who are you? The pursuit can become another form of running
growthseeker.description: Growth Seekers have deep passion for learning and development — sometimes making improvement itself another form of running.

questions.identity: When you improve something about yourself, what old self are you trying to leave behind?
questions.memory: Describe a growth goal that became more important than the life you were living.
questions.unsaid: What if you didn't need to become anyone else — what would you do with that freedom?
questions.fear: What part of yourself have you been trying to fix or improve for so long that you've forgotten it might just be who you are?

growthseeker.image_direction: A garden tended obsessively, every plant staked and pruned, one wild bloom in the corner left alone
growthseeker.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

protector.name: Protector
protector.tagline: Upholds traditions, rules, standards, and personal duty
protector.core_wound: The rules they protect so fiercely are sometimes protecting them from having to change
protector.description: Protectors uphold traditions, rules, and standards — sometimes using duty as protection from having to change.

questions.identity: What do you protect that also protects you from having to grow?
questions.memory: Describe a time you held the line and wondered afterward who it was really for.
questions.unsaid: What rule would you break if no one you respect was watching?
questions.fear: What is something you have defended strongly — a belief, a standard, a way of doing things — that you have privately started to doubt?

protector.image_direction: An old wall, beautifully maintained, the landscape it was built to protect long since changed around it
protector.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

enforcer.name: Enforcer
enforcer.tagline: Engages through standards, rules, and traditions
enforcer.core_wound: Standards as a measuring stick they inevitably hold against themselves first
enforcer.description: Enforcers engage through standards and rules — inevitably holding the measuring stick against themselves first.

questions.identity: When you enforce a standard, whose approval are you secretly seeking?
questions.memory: Describe a time you held someone accountable and recognized the same flaw in yourself.
questions.unsaid: What standard do you enforce in others because you can't enforce it in yourself?
questions.fear: By your own standards — the ones you hold others to — where are you currently failing yourself, and what have you told yourself about why that's different?

enforcer.image_direction: A judge's scale, perfectly balanced, with one small stone hidden under the judge's own side
enforcer.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

critic.name: Critic
critic.core_wound: The sharpest critiques are often the ones they haven't yet aimed inward
critic.description: Critics express opinions and debate perspectives — often wielding sharpest critiques they haven't yet aimed inward.

questions.identity: When you spot what's wrong, what does that clarity protect you from feeling?
questions.memory: Describe a critique you gave that landed true — and stung because you recognized yourself.
questions.unsaid: What flaw in yourself do you keep finding in other people?
questions.fear: What critique do you give others frequently that, if you turned it fully on yourself, would be the most uncomfortable thing you could hear?

critic.image_direction: A mirror cracked at the center, reflecting everything perfectly except the face looking into it
critic.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text

individualist.name: Individualist
individualist.tagline: Marches to their own beat with unique self-expression
individualist.core_wound: Originality as armor — being different is safe; being ordinary and vulnerable is terrifying
individualist.description: Individualists march to their own beat with unique self-expression — sometimes using originality as armor against ordinary vulnerability.

questions.identity: What part of being different feels most authentically you — and what part is performance?
questions.memory: Describe a time you chose the unique path and secretly wished someone would walk it with you.
questions.unsaid: What ordinary desire have you disguised as individuality?
questions.fear: Underneath the part of you that insists on being different — what do you want that is embarrassingly simple and ordinary?

individualist.image_direction: A figure walking a completely unique path through a field, glancing sideways at the ordinary road they've been avoiding
individualist.image_style: cinematic graphic novel, ink illustration, dramatic shadows, no speech bubbles, no text
